title: cerastium alpinum var. atratum
intro: Cerastium alpinum var. atratum, also known as the purple mountain chickweed, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe and calm the skin and reduce the appearance of wounds and burns. The health benefits of Cerastium alpinum var. atratum include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Cerastium alpinum var. atratum can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
